message:
fix(command_guard): strip safe patterns before destructive matching

Refactor CommandGuard evaluation to strip safe patterns from the
command string before checking destructive patterns, instead of
short-circuiting the entire pack on first safe match.

Previous behavior: If ANY safe rule matched the command, the entire
rule pack was skipped. This meant compound commands like
'rm -rf target; rm -rf /' would be classified as safe if 'rm -rf target'
matched a safe pattern, even though 'rm -rf /' is destructive.

New behavior: Safe rules are applied as regex replacements that remove
the safe portions from the command string. The remaining text is then
checked against destructive patterns. This correctly catches
destructive subcommands hidden after safe prefixes.

Applied identically to both CommandGuard::evaluate() (stateful) and
evaluate_stateless() (free function) to maintain behavioral parity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
